key_insight: A multi-rank gut microbiome feature model found a measurable baseline signal for anti-PD-1 response in hepatocellular carcinoma, and the trained network was compressed into a lookup-table predictor for practical deployment.
clinical_relevance: If externally validated, this approach could help identify likely responders before treatment starts and support earlier, more personalized immunotherapy planning in HCC.
method: Reanalyzed 16S rRNA data from 35 HCC patients with 155 longitudinal samples.
Used multi-rank taxonomic pooling, log-transformation, and strict subject-level train-test splits to reduce leakage and preserve weak signals.
Distilled the neural network into a compact LUT with frozen centered log-ratio normalization and interpolation for standalone inference.
evidence_signal: An 8-taxon neural network reached AUROC 0.784 ± 0.024 on held-out data and still separated responders from non-responders at baseline (AUROC 0.681 ± 0.056).
watch_item: The baseline signal is promising but based on a small cohort and still needs prospective, external validation before clinical use.
paper_title: Early Prediction of Anti-PD-1 Response in Hepatocellular Carcinoma via Multi-Rank Taxonomic Feature Engineering and a LUT-Based Prediction Vehicle
